chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1360 of 2023 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on Misc. No.4037/2025 2. For orders on Misc. No.4038/2025 3. For orders on Misc. No.1826/2025 4. For orders on Misc. No.28432/2024 5. For orders on Misc. No.23279/2023 6. For orders on Misc. No.22800/2023 7. For orders as to maintainability of petition 26.02.2025 Mr. Abdul Latif Leghari, advocate for the petitioner Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General Mr. Sandeep Malani, Assistant Advocate General Sindh 1. Granted. 2-7. Present petition assails a letter dated 22.02.2023 regarding implementation of decision of PN&MC. It is apparent that no decision in itself has been challenged and it is only this letter that seeks implementation thereto that is the subject matter of present lis. No case is made out to warrant interference in writ jurisdiction and even otherwise learned counsel has remained unable to befall the petitioner in the category of an aggrieved person per Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Petition, listed for maintainability since inception, found to be misconceived, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Amjad
